examples.title: Thread Pool Implementation using Pthreads
examples.description: Demonstrates thread pool creation, task submission, and worker thread execution in C using POSIX threads
examples.explanation: This code implements a thread pool where a fixed number of worker threads are created once and reused to execute multiple tasks. The thread pool uses a synchronized queue to distribute work among threads.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for n tasks with n workers - each task O(1) to enqueue/dequeue
examples.spaceComplexity: O(w + q) where w = number of worker threads, q = queue size

examples.title: Threading Example in Python
examples.description: Demonstrates creating threads and using a thread pool in Python
examples.code:
import threading
import queue
import time

# Task function
def worker_task(task_id, delay):
    time.sleep(delay)
    print(f"Task {task_id} completed by {threading.current_thread().name}")

# Method 1: Creating individual threads
def create_threads():
    threads = []
    for i in range(5):
        t = threading.Thread(target=worker_task, args=(i, 1))
        threads.append(t)
        t.start()
    
    for t in threads:
        t.join()

# Method 2: Using ThreadPoolExecutor
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor

def use_thread_pool():
    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=3) as executor:
        for i in range(5):
            executor.submit(worker_task, i, 1)

# Demonstrate thread synchronization
counter = 0
lock = threading.Lock()

def increment_with_lock():
    global counter
    with lock:
        temp = counter
        time.sleep(0.1)
        counter = temp + 1

def demonstrate_sync():
    threads = [threading.Thread(target=increment_with_lock) for _ in range(10)]
    for t in threads:
        t.start()
    for t in threads:
        t.join()
    print(f"Counter value: {counter}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    print("=== Individual Threads ===")
    create_threads()
    
    print("\n=== Thread Pool ===")
    use_thread_pool()
    
    print("\n=== Synchronization Demo ===")
    demonstrate_sync()
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates three threading concepts: creating individual threads, using thread pool with ThreadPoolExecutor for efficient resource management, and proper synchronization using locks to prevent race conditions.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for n tasks with k workers - work is distributed
examples.spaceComplexity: O(k) where k = number of worker threads in pool

examples.title: Thread Models in Java
examples.description: Demonstrates One-to-One model implementation and thread pool in Java
examples.explanation: Java uses One-to-One model where each Java thread corresponds to a native OS thread. The Executor framework provides thread pooling (conceptually Many-to-Many). Synchronized keyword ensures thread-safe access to shared resources.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) tasks distributed across k threads - O(n/k) per thread
examples.spaceComplexity: O(k) for thread pool + O(1) per task
